entry.title: Debugging Ray Tracing Applications Using NVIDIA OptiX Toolkit | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: NVIDIA OptiX ray tracing engine is an application framework for achieving optimal ray tracing performance on the GPU. Applications using OptiX can fail in ways that are difficult to diagnose: an…

entry.title: Start Customizing NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano with Prime Intellect Lab in Minutes | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: Customization is what enables developers to take a general model and tailor it to use cases, domains, languages, and more. However, customization comes with a few challenges.

entry.title: GeForce NOW Sets Sail With ‘Path of Exile: Curse of the Allflame’ Joining the Cloud
entry.summary: Plus, ‘Battlefield 6’ updates, Capcom classics, ‘Halo: Campaign Evolved Advanced Access’ and nine new games arrive on the cloud this week.

entry.title: NVIDIA AI Supercomputer Comes Online at Naval Postgraduate School
entry.summary: NVIDIA founder and CEO Jensen Huang joined school leadership in Monterey, California, to commission the system, which will fuel AI education and research at the US military’s premier technical graduate university.

entry.title: Make Long-Running NVIDIA TensorRT Engine Builds Observable and Cancelable in Python or C++ | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: A TensorRT engine build can take seconds to many minutes. Large strongly typed models, deep tactic search, and a cold timing cache on a brand-new GPU SKU can leave developers, end users…

entry.title: NVIDIA Open Sources First GPU-Accelerated Medical Physics Simulation Framework
entry.summary: Medical device leaders are already building on NVIDIA’s new Medical Physics Simulation framework — giving surgical robots the virtual experience they need before interacting with patients.

entry.title: 3 Google updates from Galaxy Unpacked 2026
entry.summary: We shared how Samsung users can boost productivity and get time back on new foldables, watches, and glasses coming soon.

entry.title: Built in Fort Worth: Wistron Opens Advanced Manufacturing Plant to Produce NVIDIA AI Systems
entry.summary: The AI era runs on AI infrastructure. Many of these advanced systems are built and tested in Texas. Wistron opened its first U.S. manufacturing facility today in Fort Worth — a 324,000-square-foot greenfield plant producing superchips at the heart of some of the world’s most capable AI systems. In front of an audience of Wistron […]

entry.title: Setting a World Record for MoE Pre-Training on NVIDIA GB300 NVL72 | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: Frontier model pre-training has converged on mixture of experts (MoE), which is fundamentally changing what limits large-scale AI training. As compute per token falls…

entry.title: Inside NVIDIA Rubin GPU Architecture: Powering the Era of Agentic AI | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: What began as discrete AI model training and human-facing chat interfaces has evolved into always-on AI factories dedicated to producing intelligence at scale. These factories are now tasked with…

entry.title: NVIDIA Vera CPU: Olympus Cores Built for Maximum Single-Thread Performance in Agentic AI | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: Agentic AI shifts more of the critical execution path onto the CPU. Agents operate in sandboxes to execute code, invoke tools, retrieve context, interact with databases, and analyze results before…

entry.title: Introducing Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber
entry.summary: Google introduces Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber to help defenders find, validate, and patch software vulnerabilities quickly and efficiently.

entry.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Driving Performance Per Watt, Lowest Token Cost for Partners Worldwide
entry.summary: Backed by 300 global partners, Vera Rubin is ramping up worldwide. NVIDIA partners CoreWeave, Google Cloud, Microsoft Azure and Mistral are among many deploying Vera Rubin, which delivers benchmark leadership on performance per watt and lowest token costs.

entry.title: Built for Vera Rubin, NVIDIA Spectrum-6 Arrives in Gigascale AI Factories
entry.summary: Next-generation NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet connects hundreds of thousands of GPUs with the performance, resilience and efficiency needed for gigascale AI, with AI infrastructure leaders already adopting.

entry.title: NVIDIA and Partners Build in America, for America
entry.summary: NVIDIA and its partners are investing in American manufacturing, supply chains, energy grids and skilled workforces so the U.S. can produce the infrastructure needed for better healthcare, breakthrough scientific discovery, stronger industrial productivity and global technology leadership.

entry.title: NVIDIA NVLink: The Scale-Up Network for AI Factories | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: The demand for AI continues to accelerate. Workloads are getting larger, models are becoming more complex, and there is mounting pressure to deploy AI compute infrastructure faster than ever.

entry.title: At SIGGRAPH, NVIDIA Advances Graphics and Simulation With Agentic and Physical AI
entry.summary: From open models to real-time simulation, AI and graphics breakthroughs are transforming media, content creation and robotics.

entry.title: Integrate NVIDIA Omniverse RTX Sensor Simulation Into Existing Apps | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: Developers building 3D, design, simulation, robotics, and industrial digital twin applications need ways to bring physical AI capabilities into the tools and services they already use.

entry.title: Bristol Myers Squibb Building Life Science Industry’s Most Advanced AI Factory on NVIDIA Vera Rubin
entry.summary: The pharmaceutical company has demonstrated how AI can transform how drugs discovery and is now scaling AI across R&D to accelerate the discovery of next-generation medicines.

entry.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Maximizes Intelligence per Dollar for Post-Training Workloads — a Key Metric for Agentic AI
entry.summary: Lowest cost per token from extreme codesign maximizes intelligence per dollar for post-training in the agentic era.

entry.title: Q&A: How Capcom Brought Path Tracing to RE ENGINE Across PRAGMATA and Resident Evil Requiem | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: Capcom’s RE ENGINE team set out to bring path tracing into two shipping titles at once, Resident Evil Requiem and PRAGMATA, each with a different visual identity. Over two years, the RE ENGINE team…

entry.title: Integrating Context-Aware Video AI Agents Into Enterprise Workflows | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: A video analytics AI agent that can perceive, reason, and act based on massive amounts of video footage must be integrated with existing workflows and applications to be useful.

entry.title: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs approach to bioresilience
entry.summary: Google DeepMind and Isomorphic Labs approach to bioresilience, using AI models to support prevention, detection and response.

entry.title: Connect more of your apps to Search
entry.summary: You’ll be able to securely link and interact with your go-to services directly in AI Mode.

entry.title: Create, edit and star in videos with two Google Vids updates
entry.summary: Gemini Omni and personal avatars in Google Vids make video creation easier than ever.

entry.title: AI in Indian Education: Atal Innovation Mission and Google launch ATL Saathi
entry.summary: Atal Innovation Mission launches ATL Saathi, a Gemini powered AI assistant empowering India's educators to nurture the next generation of innovators.

entry.title: Celebrating 25 years of visual search innovation
entry.summary: Google Images is turning 25. Here’s a look back at some major milestones — and new ways to explore and create visual content.

entry.title: Scaling cryptographic verification to boost computer security
entry.summary: Cryptographic code supports vital protections in modern computing systems. Learn how a new method helps verify code as developers write it while preserving speed and adaptability as it gets implemented and evolves:

entry.title: Amazon and University of Michigan give robots a sense of touch
entry.summary: HydroShear, a new physics-based simulator, teaches robots how to use their sense of touch to perform complex manipulation tasks, in a way that transfers seamlessly to the real world.

entry.title: Aurora 1.5: Extending open foundation models for weather and Earth-system applications - Microsoft Research
entry.summary: Aurora 1.5 adds 22 more variables, hourly temporal resolution, and probabilistic ensemble forecasting to the Aurora foundation model, making it more useful for real-world weather, climate, and energy applications.

entry.title: Capturing token IDs during agentic interactions for better reinforcement learning
entry.summary: A new Rust proxy called Turnstile sits between the model backend and the agent harness to capture information lost in mere text transcripts.

entry.title: Create a LangChain Deep Agents Harness Profile for NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra to Improve Performance | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: Agentic systems often face a trade-off between accuracy and cost. The highest-performing proprietary frontier models and harnesses provide top accuracy but are expensive. Fine-tuning offers one way to…

entry.title: AI can generate Charts. Flint helps generate better ones.
entry.summary: Short chart specifications are easy to write, but often produce uninspiring results. Flint is an open-source visualization language that offers a middle path, letting AI agents create expressive charts from compact, human-editable specifications.

entry.title: NVIDIA Vera CPU Boosts AI Factory Throughput to Accelerate Agentic Workloads | NVIDIA Technical Blog
entry.summary: Agentic systems turn model reasoning into action through multi-step workflows that combine inference, tool use, code execution, retrieval, orchestration, and result handling. As these systems scale…

entry.title: Expanding Managed Agents in Gemini API: background tasks, remote MCP and more
entry.summary: We’re announcing new capabilities in Managed Agents in Gemini API so developers can build reliable, production-ready agents.

entry.title: The latest AI news we announced in June 2026
entry.summary: Here are Google’s latest AI updates from June 2026.

entry.title: New York City educators and industry leaders gathered at Google’s offices to shape the future of AI in classrooms.
entry.summary: Google, the New York Jobs CEO Council and Urban Assembly hosted an AI summit for 150 education and industry leaders.

entry.title: SkillOpt turns AI agent skills into trainable assets
entry.summary: AI agents often fail because their instructions, or skills, are manually modified with no guarantee of improvement. Learn how SkillOpt turns skill editing into a training process, making agent behavior more reliable without changing model weights:

entry.title: Unlocking Britain’s next era of productivity: Building a nation of AI trailblazers
entry.summary: Google UK shares its latest Economic Impact Report and how to enable more people to unlock the benefits of AI-powered technologies.

entry.title: Memora scales agent memory to boost long-horizon productivity
entry.summary: AI agents can't remember past conversations. They must constantly reload or retrieve context, which grows less efficient as tasks get longer and more complex. Memora solves this with a scalable memory system separating what’s stored from how it's retrieved.

entry.title: Ask an AI expert: What exactly is the full stack?
entry.summary: A Google expert explains what it means to take a full-stack approach to AI and why it’s been the foundation of our AI work for so long.

entry.title: Turning brain prediction models into testable explanations
entry.summary: Researchers introduce generative causal testing, which translates black box models into clear hypotheses and verifies them in the scanner, revealing what specific brain regions respond to in language.

entry.title: Our latest Google Finance upgrades, including a new app
entry.summary: The new Google Finance is coming out of beta and launching a new Android app.

entry.title: The fuel of the future is already here: Why TRISO matters
entry.summary: Millimeter-scale particles of nuclear-reactor fuel are encased in four layers of different materials that act as a “miniature containment system”.

entry.title: Talos brings continuous genomic reanalysis to nearly 5,000 unsolved cases
entry.summary: Talos was built to help resolve a major bottleneck in genomic medicine: human review time. The open-source system recovered 90% of in-scope diagnoses while surfacing just 1.3 candidate variants per patient for expert review.

entry.title: Securing internal systems against increasingly capable and imperfectly aligned AI
entry.summary: Discover our AI Control Roadmap: a defense-in-depth system to securely manage advanced, potentially misaligned AI agents.

entry.title: Unlocking UK house-building with AI-accelerated planning
entry.summary: Google DeepMind is working alongside the UK government to co-develop an AI-powered prototype to help cut application decision times by 50%.

entry.title: Inside Project Ire’s discovery of an evasive malware sample
entry.summary: Project Ire examined a timely malware sample and determined its intent through reverse engineering—identifying LOTUSLITE characteristics even as most major EDR tools did not detect it.

entry.title: Google DeepMind and partners announce multi-agent safety research funding call.
entry.summary: Google DeepMind and partners are announcing a new technical research funding call of up to $10M for researchers worldwide to strengthen multi-agent safety.

entry.title: Graviton5’s improved design increases speed and energy efficiency — beyond Moore’s law
entry.summary: A new chiplet architecture, custom die-to-die connectivity, and support for DDR5-8800 memory and the latest PCIe gen6 interconnects improve performance by 25% for general-purpose and agentic AI workloads.

entry.title: EC2’s formally verified “isolation engine” provides mathematical assurance of virtual-machine isolation
entry.summary: Splitting the “separation kernel” off from the rest of the Nitro security system and using only a subset of the Rust programming language to code it enabled its formal verification.

entry.title: Real-world grounding in agentic AI
entry.summary: Four approaches can dramatically improve the performance and trustworthiness of AI agents in operational environments.

entry.title: Bridging intent and execution in agentic systems
entry.summary: The harnesses that mediate between models and tools in agentic systems are becoming their own performance bottleneck, but a few simple design principles can fix what ails them.

entry.title: Data Formulator 0.7: AI-powered data analytics for enterprise data
entry.summary: Data Formulator introduces AI-powered analytics for enterprise data workflows. Data teams can easily bring enterprise data into an AI-ready workspace where users can explore, analyze, and visualize data with AI agents to turn raw data into actionable insights:

entry.title: Extending human intelligence through AI
entry.summary: Understanding AI as an extension of human intelligence—not a replacement for it—offers a more grounded path for building trustworthy AI systems.

entry.title: AWS and Gray Lab at Johns Hopkins Whiting School of Engineering announce groundbreaking database for AI/ML antibody design
entry.summary: The Antibody Developability Benchmark is powered by one of the most diverse antibody datasets, enabling transparent performance evaluation for AI-guided antibody design.

entry.title: How AI is changing the nature of mathematical research
entry.summary: What machine learning theorists learned using AI agents to generate proofs — and what comes next.

entry.title: SIMA 2: A Gemini-Powered AI Agent for 3D Virtual Worlds
entry.summary: Introducing SIMA 2, the next milestone in our research creating general and helpful AI agents. By integrating the advanced capabilities of our Gemini models, SIMA is evolving from an instruction-follower into an interactive gaming companion.

entry.title: Autonomous Swarming Takes Flight: The Next Era of UAV Operations
entry.summary: How Palladyne AI's edge-based swarming redefines UAV operations. True drone teamwork that boosts agility, resilience, and mission efficiency.

entry.title: Amazon and Carnegie Mellon University launch strategic AI Innovation Hub
entry.summary: The collaboration will advance research in generative AI, robotics, natural-language processing and cloud computing while fostering innovation in foundational and emerging technologies.
